intro: Aleuritia smithiana, also known as Aleurites, is a species of plant in the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae), characterized by its bright red seeds and large, palmate leaves. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and skin diseases, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Aleurites include aleuritic acid, which is responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. Aleurites is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are applied topically to soothe skin irritations and other conditions.
